title: leptosiphon septentrionalis
intro: Leptosiphon septentrionalis, also known as the Northern cluster lily or Mountain bluebells, is a small, perennial herbaceous plant native to the western coast of North America. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Consuming Leptosiphon septentrionalis may have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the flowers in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant in a salve or tincture to apply topically.
